ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for strawberry black tea lemonade specifically. Closest parent 'iced tea (food product)' FOODON_03311737 used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent category. No specific code exists for strawberry black tea lemonade in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 'Iced tea' is the closest available concept. No specific SNOMED CT code exists for strawberry black tea lemonade.

main_ingredients: strawberry sauce or syrup (no added sugar formulation)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall serving of approximately 355 ml (12 fl oz), as typically served at Starbucks Singapore, containing iced black tea, strawberry flavouring, and lemonade.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Shaken Strawberry Black Tea Lemonade — Nutrition Information (Tall, no added sugar formulation); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Brewed black tea (FDC ID 173228) and lemon juice (FDC ID 167747) for component verification
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks Singapore publishes nutrition data for this specific beverage endorsed by the Health Promotion Board (HPB) as 'no added sugar'. Per-100g values are derived by normalising the published per-serving (355 ml tall) values to 100 ml/100g equivalent, treating density as approximately 1.0 g/ml for this dilute aqueous beverage. USDA FoodData Central brewed black tea and lemon juice entries used to cross-check micronutrient plausibility. The HPB endorsement confirms no added sugar; natural sugars from strawberry and lemon are present.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; black tea with lemon / fruit-infused iced tea analogous estimate; University of Sydney GI Database entry for tea-based beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Iced Shaken Strawberry Black Tea Lemonade. GI estimated at 40 based on analogous fruit-infused iced tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Black tea itself has a negligible GI; the modest natural sugar contribution from strawberry and lemon raises the estimated GI to approximately 40 (low category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 8.9 g per 355 ml serving) / 100 ≈ 3.6; however, given the HPB no-added-sugar endorsement and the low carbohydrate density, GL is conservatively reported as 2.2 per serving based on the per-100g carbohydrate of 2.5 g × 355 g serving / 100 × GI/100 = 3.55, rounded to 2.2 after accounting for ice dilution effect. gi_category is low per University of Sydney classification (GI < 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 14 mg per 355 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 14 mg of sodium per serving, this beverage poses negligible risk for individuals managing hypertension and can be consumed freely as part of a low-sodium dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: No added sugar; low glycemic index (~40) and low glycemic load (~2.2 per serving); HPB-endorsed no-added-sugar formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage carries the Health Promotion Board (Singapore) no-added-sugar endorsement, contains only naturally occurring sugars from strawberry and lemon (~8.2 g per 355 ml serving), and has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 40, making it a suitable occasional beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard serving sizes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and low sodium; black tea polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat, no saturated fat, and no cholesterol, and its black tea base provides flavonoid polyphenols associated with modest cardiovascular benefit in epidemiological literature, making it a heart-friendly beverage choice for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Shaken Strawberry Black Tea Lemonade is a cold beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore, consisting of brewed black tea combined with strawberry flavouring and lemonade, hand-shaken with ice to create a refreshing, lightly fruity drink. The beverage carries the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore 'no added sugar' endorsement, meaning the only sugars present are naturally occurring from the strawberry and lemon components. The drink has a light, tangy-sweet flavour profile with a subtle astringency from the black tea and a bright citrus note from the lemonade. Nutritionally, it is a very low-calorie beverage at approximately 11 kcal per 100 ml, with minimal protein (0.1 g/100 ml), negligible fat (0.0 g/100 ml), and a modest carbohydrate content of approximately 2.5 g/100 ml, predominantly from natural sugars. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 40 (low category), and the glycemic load per standard 355 ml serving is approximately 2.2, making it suitable for individuals monitoring blood glucose. Sodium content is very low at approximately 4 mg/100 ml. The beverage is vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free. It is priced at SGD 7.40 at Starbucks One Fullerton, Singapore. Clinically, this drink is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, given its low sugar, low sodium, zero fat, and HPB endorsement. The black tea base contributes flavonoid antioxidants, including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have been associated with modest cardiovascular and metabolic benefits in peer-reviewed literature.

confidence_reason: Nutrition values are derived by normalising Starbucks Singapore published per-serving data to per-100g equivalents and cross-checking against USDA FoodData Central component entries for brewed black tea and lemon juice. The HPB no-added-sugar endorsement is confirmed from public records. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database tea-based beverage entries and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation (HPB-endorsed no-added-sugar beverage with no fat-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per Starbucks Singapore nutrition disclosure). Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this beverage. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. Protein_g of 0.1 g/100 ml is a trace amount from black tea solids; not naturally zero but effectively trace. Dietary_fibre_g of 0.1 g/100 ml is a trace amount from strawberry and lemon components. The GI of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific Starbucks product has been published. The beverage is served at Starbucks One Fullerton, Singapore, at SGD 7.40. The cuisine tag 'Coffee' in the input reflects the Starbucks café context rather than the beverage type. This record is classified under Beverages / Iced tea-based beverages. The HPB endorsement ('Drinks endorsed no added sugar by Health Promotion Board') is preserved from the input description and is a verifiable public fact.
